reasoning: To achieve 18 consecutive weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard 200, "The Life of a Showgirl" would have to hold the top spot from its debut on the October 18, 2025 chart all the way through mid-February 2026. While the album has had an incredibly strong start (5 weeks at No. 1 with a record-breaking 4 million first-week debut), its sales have naturally decelerated (down to 120K in week 5). It will soon face the grueling December holiday season, where massive perennial favorites like Michael Bublé's 'Christmas' and Mariah Carey surge to the top of the charts with 100K+ equivalent units. Even Taylor Swift's previous behemoth, 'The Tortured Poets Department', only managed 12 consecutive weeks before being briefly displaced. With the relentless decay of album units over a 4+ month span and the seasonal obstacle of December holiday releases, a historic 18-week uninterrupted streak is highly improbable.
